overview:
## Discussion

A rule system calls this method when evaluating its rules, if and only if the rule’s predicate has been satisfied (that is, the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/evaluatePredicate(in:)] method has returned [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true]), and after moving the rule from its [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/agenda] list to its [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/executed] list.

If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:assertingFact:grade:)] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:retractingFact:grade:)], calling this method asserts or retracts the fact as specified in the provided rule system. If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(blockPredicate:action:)] method, calling this method calls the action block. Otherwise, this method does nothing—subclasses should override this method to implement their own actions.

# performAction(in:)

**Instance Method**

Performs actions that should result when the rule is satisfied in the context of the specified rule system.

## Declaration

```swift
func performAction(in system: GKRuleSystem)
```

## Discussion

A rule system calls this method when evaluating its rules, if and only if the rule’s predicate has been satisfied (that is, the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/evaluatePredicate(in:)] method has returned [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true]), and after moving the rule from its [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/agenda] list to its [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRuleSystem/executed] list.

If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:assertingFact:grade:)] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(predicate:retractingFact:grade:)], calling this method asserts or retracts the fact as specified in the provided rule system. If the rule was created with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRule/init(blockPredicate:action:)] method, calling this method calls the action block. Otherwise, this method does nothing—subclasses should override this method to implement their own actions.

## Evaluating a Rule

- **evaluatePredicate(in:)**: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the rule has been satisfied in the context of the specified rule system.
